title: ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. filicaulis
intro: Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. filicaulis, also known as the water crowfoot or long-beaked water crowfoot, is a subspecies of flowering plant that belongs to the buttercup family (Ranunculaceae). This aquatic plant is native to Europe and is known for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to stimulate the liver and kidneys, and its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus trichophyllus subsp. filicaulis include its potential to treat conditions such as jaundice, liver and kidney disease, and skin conditions like eczema and acne. In herbal preparations, the plant is often used in the form of infusions, tinctures, and salves, and is typically consumed in moderation due to its potential toxicity when ingested in large quantities.
